title: festuca rubra subsp. vulgaris
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. vulgaris, also known as red fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of red fescue include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of red fescue can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional practices as a poultice or compress to treat skin conditions and wounds.
